title: Codex Local Profile Import Workaround Tasks
principles: Keep the original hosted OAuth blocker intact; this is a local trusted-operator workaround only.
Import only an access token, never a refresh or ID token, and fail closed at expiry.
Keep local cache reads in @bundjil/codex-oauth and out of Vercel, Eve, routes, CI, and browser code.
Use canonical Effect Schema contracts, Context services, Layers, Config, Config.redacted, and KeyValueStore.
Do not inspect, print, commit, test against, or document real credential values.
globalVerification: Sanitized-only output and leak scans for every local or preview proof

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: The parent must complete and record distinct ownership/call-graph, Effect implementation-quality, and verification-coverage audits before accepting each task. More passes are mandatory when a finding remains.

tasks.title: Implement the trusted-local access-token profile importer
tasks.description: Add package-owned local cache source, importer service, config, sanitized CLI command, fixture tests, and package documentation.
implementationNotes: Decode a minimal internal Codex CLI cache schema with Effect Schema; reject non-chatgpt modes and do not parse opaque JWT claims.
Read the cache only after config for profile identity, encryption, and Upstash is valid.
Construct an explicit CodexOAuthProfile with accessToken only, requiresReauthentication true, and no refreshToken property.
Do not use process.env directly, JSON.stringify, manual JSON parsing, unsafe casts, or a script-local DTO/parser.
Expose a fixture/memory source layer for deterministic tests. The live source must be command-only and unavailable to proxy composition.
Return schema-backed sanitized metadata, never cache contents, token values/lengths, account ids, or paths unless a path is explicitly safe.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen importer/source/config programs with typed errors handled in .pipe(...) using catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Schema-derived types and tagged errors from @bundjil/codex-oauth; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers.
Fixture tests cover valid cache, missing access token, wrong auth mode, malformed cache, expired token, no refresh/id-token persistence, encrypted-store result, and sanitized result/error output.
Run the command only against an invalid fixture/path as automated proof; do not use real credentials in test output.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run verification
completionCriteria: A local operator can run a documented command with ignored local configuration to encrypt and store an access-token-only profile.
No deployed app layer imports or can resolve the local auth-cache source.

tasks.title: Compose access-only live proxy mode
tasks.description: Wire apps/codex-proxy live mode to encrypted Upstash profiles and the direct Codex provider while preserving mock mode.
implementationNotes: Compose live dependencies through explicit layers after Effect config decoding; do not introduce import-time environment branching.
Use CodexOAuthService.getValidToken only. Never call refreshAccessToken for this workaround.
Map missing/expired profiles and unavailable live config to safe non-secret 502 responses with a re-import instruction.
Keep route semantics, Vercel entrypoint, mock response, and internal bearer protection intact.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen route/layer composition and typed .pipe(...) error mapping.
Canonical app/package schemas, tagged errors, Config, explicit layers, and no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers.
Tests cover mock preservation, live profile stream via mocked fetch, missing profile, expired profile, missing live config, no API-key fallback, auth failures, and leak-safe errors.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy smoke-test
bun run verification
completionCriteria: Live proxy composition uses only encrypted access-token profiles and fails closed after expiry.
Mock remains the default and continues to pass its local smoke test.

tasks.title: Prove the personal Vercel preview workaround
tasks.description: Provision preview-only personal Vercel/Upstash configuration, perform the opt-in local import, and verify a live proxy stream with sanitized evidence.
implementationNotes: Verify personal Vercel scope and project before changing preview env. Do not use Tilt Legal resources.
Do not set production env or deploy production.
First validate an invalid importer fixture/path. Perform the real import only after the user has local Codex login and ignored env configuration ready.
Record status/mode/content type/stream markers/leak booleans only; do not record model contents, credentials, account ids, cache paths, or raw Upstash values.
mandatoryVerification: Personal Vercel project/scope and preview-only environment variable names are confirmed without values.
Upstash readback proves the profile value is ciphertext-only and lacks refresh/id-token data.
Health, unauthenticated, invalid-internal-token, missing/expired-profile, and authenticated live stream probes are recorded in sanitized form.
Vercel logs are scanned for token/cache/prompt/raw-response leakage.
bun run verification
completionCriteria: A personal Vercel preview has a recorded live access-token-only proof or a precise external blocker.

tasks.title: Document workaround operation and rollback
tasks.description: Make the current implementation, expiry/re-import workflow, self-test procedure, preview proof, and rollback discoverable without claiming hosted OAuth support.
implementationNotes: Update READMEs and architecture docs named in the SPEC.
Add a concise operator runbook for local import, preview checks, expiry recovery, and switching back to mock mode.
Keep the blocked hosted OAuth task linked and distinct from the workaround.
mandatoryVerification: Docs accurately distinguish mock mode, access-token-only workaround, blocked hosted OAuth, and future Eve integration.
Examples use only env variable names and sanitized expected output.
Effect requirements, schema JSON boundary rules, and the completed 3-pass audit evidence remain documented.
bun run verification
completionCriteria: A future operator can set up, test, refresh by re-import, inspect safely, and roll back without reading task history.
